demoRev1aBody: Shoulders sit perfectly and the wool has a nice drape. Wore it to a client dinner and got compliments.
demoRev1bBody: Great value. Needed a quick hem on the trousers—otherwise ready to wear.
demoRev2aBody: Charcoal reads well in photos and in person. Fabric breathes on warmer days.
demoRev2bBody: Happy with the fit. Jacket sleeves were spot-on for my arm length.
demoRev2cBody: Looks expensive without feeling stiff. Wears well with brown or black shoes.
demoRev3aBody: Works for daytime events and dressed-down with a knit. Color is true to the site.
demoRev3bBody: Classic cut. I’d size up if you prefer a roomier jacket.
demoRev4aBody: Peak lapels and satin trim look formal without feeling costume-y. Packed well for travel.
demoRev4bBody: Black is deep and even under indoor lighting. Waist suppression is flattering.
demoRev5aBody: Texture hides wrinkles better than plain cloth. Turned heads at a spring gala.
demoRev5bBody: Emerald is richer than my monitor showed—in a good way. Lining feels smooth.
demoRev6aBody: Ivory reads elegant, not stark. Lightweight enough for outdoor photos.
demoRev6bBody: Blazer and trousers match perfectly. Dry clean only as expected.
demoRev7aBody: Waistcoat adds structure. Brown tone pairs with burgundy tie and oxfords.
demoRev7bBody: Solid construction. Vest buttons align cleanly with the jacket.
demoRev8aBody: Midnight reads almost black in dim light—perfect for cocktail dress codes.
demoRev8bBody: Silhouette is trim but I could still move comfortably on the dance floor.
